briefdescription.para: The up direction.
The down direction.
The number of supported directions.
Value to describe directions for ContentIndicator.
Function signature for the .content_offset_changed_handler callback.
Configures a ContentIndicator for the given direction.

parameterdescription.para: The ContentIndicator to configure.
The direction for which to configure the ContentIndicator.
The configuration to use to configure the ContentIndicator. If NULL, the data for the specified direction will be reset.

simplesect.para: True if the ContentIndicator was successfully configured for the given direction, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Creates a ContentIndicator on the heap.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the ContentIndicator. NULL if the ContentIndicator could not be created.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a ContentIndicator previously created using content_indicator_create().

parameterdescription.para: The ContentIndicator to destroy.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the availability status of content in the given direction.

parameterdescription.para: The ContentIndicator for which to get the content availability.
The direction for which to get the content availability.

simplesect.para: True if content is available in the given direction, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets the availability status of content in the given direction.

parameterdescription.para: The ContentIndicator for which to set the content availability.
The direction for which to set the content availability.
Whether or not content is available.

simplesect.para: If times_out is enabled, calling this function resets any previously scheduled timeout timer for the ContentIndicator.

briefdescription.para: Adds the child layer to the content sub-layer of the ScrollLayer. This will make the child layer part of the scrollable contents. The content sub-layer of the ScrollLayer will become the parent of the child layer.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer to which to add the child layer.
The Layer to add to the content sub-layer of the ScrollLayer.

simplesect.para: You may need to update the size of the scrollable contents using scroll_layer_set_content_size().

briefdescription.para: Creates a new ScrollLayer on the heap and initalizes it with the default values:

listitem.para:
Callback context: NULL
A pointer to the ScrollLayer. NULL if the ScrollLayer could not be created

briefdescription.para: Destroys a ScrollLayer previously created by scroll_layer_create.
Gets the ContentIndicator for a ScrollLayer.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to get the ContentIndicator

simplesect.para: A pointer to the ContentIndicator, or NULL upon failure.

briefdescription.para: Gets the point by which the contents are offset.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to get the content offset

briefdescription.para: Gets the size of the contents layer.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to get the content size

briefdescription.para: Gets the "root" Layer of the scroll layer, which is the parent for the sub- layers used for its implementation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the ScrollLayer for which to get the "root" Layer

simplesect.para: The "root" Layer of the scroll layer.

briefdescription.para: Check whether or not the ScrollLayer uses paging when pressing buttons.

parameterdescription.para: The scroll layer for which to get the paging behavior.

simplesect.para: True, if paging is enabled; false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the visibility of the scroll layer shadow.

parameterdescription.para: The scroll layer for which to get the visibility

simplesect.para: True if the shadow is hidden, false if it is not hidden.

briefdescription.para: The click handlers for the DOWN button that the scroll layer will install as part of scroll_layer_set_click_config_onto_window().

simplesect.para: This handler is exposed, in case one wants to implement an alternative handler for the DOWN button, as a way to invoke the default behavior.

parameterdescription.para: The click recognizer for which the handler is called
A void pointer to the ScrollLayer that is the context of the click event

briefdescription.para: The click handlers for the UP button that the scroll layer will install as part of scroll_layer_set_click_config_onto_window().

simplesect.para: This handler is exposed, in case one wants to implement an alternative handler for the UP button, as a way to invoke the default behavior.

parameterdescription.para: The click recognizer for which the handler is called
A void pointer to the ScrollLayer that is the context of the click event

briefdescription.para: Sets the callbacks that the scroll layer exposes. The context as set by scroll_layer_set_context() is passed into each of the callbacks. See ScrollLayerCallbacks for the different callbacks.

simplesect.para: If the context is NULL, a pointer to scroll_layer is used as context parameter instead when calling callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to assign new callbacks.
The new callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to set the ClickConfigProvider callback on the given window to scroll layer's internal click config provider. This internal click configuration provider, will set up the default UP & DOWN scrolling behavior. This function calls window_set_click_config_provider_with_context to accomplish this.

detaileddescription.para:
If you application has set a .click_config_provider callback using scroll_layer_set_callbacks(), this will be called by the internal click config provider, after configuring the UP & DOWN buttons. This allows your application to configure the SELECT button behavior and optionally override the UP & DOWN button behavior. The callback context for the SELECT click recognizer is automatically set to the scroll layer's context (see scroll_layer_set_context() ). This context is passed into ClickHandler callbacks. For the UP and DOWN buttons, the scroll layer itself is passed in by default as the callback context in order to deal with those buttons presses to scroll up and down automatically.
scroll_layer
The ScrollLayer that needs to receive click events.
window
The window for which to set the click configuration.
Clicks
window_set_click_config_provider_with_context

briefdescription.para: Scrolls to the given offset, optionally animated.

simplesect.para: When scrolling down, the offset's .y decrements. When scrolling up, the offset's .y increments. If scrolled completely to the top, the offset is GPointZero.
The .x field must be 0. Horizontal scrolling is not supported.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to set the content offset
The final content offset
Pass in true to animate to the new content offset, or false to set the new content offset without animating.

briefdescription.para: Sets the size of the contents layer. This determines the area that is scrollable. At the moment, this needs to be set "manually" and is not derived from the geometry of the contents layers.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to set the content size.
The new content size.

briefdescription.para: Sets a new callback context. This context is passed into the scroll layer's callbacks and also the ClickHandler for the SELECT button. If NULL or not set, the context defaults to a pointer to the ScrollLayer itself.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to assign the new callback context.
The new callback context.

briefdescription.para: Set the frame of the scroll layer and adjusts the internal layers' geometry accordingly. The scroll layer is marked dirty automatically.

parameterdescription.para: The ScrollLayer for which to set the frame
The new frame

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables paging of the ScrollLayer (default: disabled). When enabled, every button press will change the scroll offset by the frame's height.

parameterdescription.para: The scroll layer for which to enable or disable paging
True, if paging should be enabled. False to enable.

briefdescription.para: Sets the visibility of the scroll layer shadow. If the visibility has changed, layer_mark_dirty() will be called automatically on the scroll layer.

parameterdescription.para: The scroll layer for which to set the shadow visibility
Supply true to make the shadow hidden, or false to make it non-hidden.

briefdescription.para: Layer that scrolls its contents, animated.

listitem.para: Facilitates vertical scrolling of a layer sub-hierarchy zero or more arbitrary layers. The example image shows a scroll layer containing one large TextLayer.
Shadows to indicate that there is more content are automatically drawn on top of the content. When the end of the scroll layer is reached, the shadow will automatically be retracted.
Scrolling from one offset to another is animated implicitly by default.

The scroll layer contains a "content" sub-layer, which is the layer that is actually moved up an down. Any layer that is a child of this "content" sub-layer, will be moved as well. Effectively, an entire layout of layers can be scrolled this way. Use the convenience function scroll_layer_add_child() to add child layers to the "content" sub-layer.

The scroll layer needs to be informed of the total size of the contents, in order to calculate from and to what point it should be able to scroll. Use scroll_layer_set_content_size() to set the size of the contents.
The button behavior is set up, using the convenience function scroll_layer_set_click_config_onto_window(). This will associate the UP and DOWN buttons with scrolling up and down.
The SELECT button can be configured by installing a click configuration provider using scroll_layer_set_callbacks().
To scroll programatically to a certain offset, use scroll_layer_set_content_offset().
It is possible to get called back for each scrolling increment, by installing the .content_offset_changed_handler callback using scroll_layer_set_callbacks().
Only vertical scrolling is supported at the moment.
